title: PET: The Most Recyclable Plastic on Earth — And Why Most of It Still Gets Wasted
excerpt: PET plastic — the material in your water bottle — is the most recycled plastic in the world. It is also one of the most wasted. Here is everything you need to know about the material at the centre of MlimaLoop's operation.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Know Your Plastics: What the Number on the Bottom of Your Bottle Actually Means
excerpt: There is a number stamped into the bottom of almost every plastic item you own. Most people have never looked at it. Here is what it means, why it matters, and what happens to each type when it is thrown away.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Plastic and People: The Human Cost of Africa's Waste Crisis
excerpt: From microplastics in human blood to communities living beside toxic dump sites, the health and social cost of plastic pollution in Africa falls hardest on those least responsible for it. This is what the data shows.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Plastic in the Ocean: How Africa's Coastlines Are Paying the Price for a Global Crisis
excerpt: From microplastics in the Indian Ocean to bleached coral reefs along the East African coast, the marine plastic crisis is devastating ecosystems that millions of people depend on. Here is what is happening beneath the surface.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Plastic on Land: How a Silent Crisis is Destroying Africa's Wildlife and Ecosystems
excerpt: From elephants with stomachs full of plastic to livestock dying in their thousands, the land-based plastic crisis across Africa is accelerating. Here is the scale of what is happening — and why it cannot wait.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: GRS Certification: Why Global Brands Can No Longer Afford to Ignore It
excerpt: The Global Recycled Standard is fast becoming the non-negotiable benchmark for recycled content claims. Here is what it means, why it matters, and what it demands from your supply chain.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Why East Africa is the next frontier for the circular economy
excerpt: The infrastructure gap, the regulatory tailwinds, and why 2026 is the moment to build East Africa's first integrated rPET recycling operation.
author: MlimaLoop Team
